thesis_status_reasoning: The system is operating under extreme stress with three pumps carrying 98.5-99.1% load (well above the 70-85% normal range) due to Pump 4 maintenance. While system pressure remains above regulatory minimums at 66.2 PSI, the sustained near-maximum pump loading creates substantial mechanical failure risk. Pump 3's elevated vibration (22.4 Hz) during high load operation (98.8%) indicates early-stage mechanical stress, though not yet at the critical 35 Hz cavitation threshold.
action_reasoning: The system remains functional but is approaching multiple risk thresholds simultaneously. Pump 3 requires continuous vibration monitoring as it operates dangerously close to both load and vibration warning levels. Any further degradation could trigger cascading failure scenarios.

key_signals.signal: All three active pumps operating at 98.5-99.1% load capacity with Pump 4 offline
Pump 3 vibration at 22.4 Hz while under 98.8% load, approaching mechanical stress indicators
System pressure maintaining 66.2 PSI, well above 40 PSI regulatory minimum
Reservoir level at 94.1% ensuring adequate supply availability
Maintenance log acknowledges elevated Pump 3 vibration and active mitigation efforts

biggest_uncertainty: The duration until Pump 4 returns to service and whether Pump 3's elevated vibration will stabilize or escalate under sustained 98.8% loading before reaching the critical 35 Hz threshold
